seo.title: Koh Samui Travel Guide: Everything You Need to Know (2026)
seo.metaDescription: Complete Koh Samui travel guide for 2026. 4-day itinerary, flights from Bangkok, ferry options, local food guide, budget breakdown, and insider tips.

intro: Koh Samui rewards both the quick visitor and the long-stayer. Four days gives you enough time for temples, waterfalls, an island day trip, and serious beach time — with evenings reserved for southern Thai seafood feasts and the famous Fisherman's Village market. This guide covers the practical stuff: how to get there without overpaying for flights, how to get around, what to eat, and how much to budget. Whether you're flying direct on Bangkok Airways or taking the budget ferry route through Surat Thani, here's everything you need.

itinerary.title: 4 Days in Koh Samui: Beaches, Temples & Island Hopping

days.title: Chaweng Beach & Temple Circuit
activities: Morning: Visit Big Buddha Temple (Wat Phra Yai) early (free) — the 12-meter golden statue is best before tour groups arrive at 10 AM
Mid-morning: Drive 5 minutes to Wat Plai Laem (free) — feed the fish in the temple lake (20 THB) and admire the 18-armed Guanyin statue
Lunch: Khao man gai at a local shophouse near Big Buddha junction (50–80 THB)
Afternoon: Hit Chaweng Beach — rent a sunbed (100–200 THB or free with a drink) and swim in the turquoise waters
Evening: Explore Chaweng Walking Street for dinner and nightlife — try the Laem Din night market for cheap street food (50–100 THB per dish)

days.title: Ang Thong Marine National Park
activities: Full day: Speedboat tour to Ang Thong Marine Park (1,800–3,500 THB including lunch and park fee)
Morning: Depart 7:30 AM from Nathon pier — first stop kayaking through emerald lagoons between limestone karsts
Midday: Hike to the Thale Nai (Emerald Lake) viewpoint — a hidden saltwater lake inside Mae Koh island
Lunch: Included BBQ lunch on one of the park's beaches
Afternoon: Snorkeling over coral reefs and swimming at pristine beaches before the return journey
Evening: Quiet dinner at your hotel or a local restaurant — you'll be pleasantly exhausted

days.title: Jungle Interior & Lamai Coast
activities: Morning: 4WD safari tour to Secret Buddha Garden (80 THB entrance + tour 800–1,500 THB) — the steep jungle drive is half the adventure
Mid-morning: Stop at Na Muang Waterfall 1 for a swim in the natural pool (free)
Lunch: Southern Thai seafood at a local restaurant near Lamai — try curried crab or yellow curry with grouper (150–300 THB)
Afternoon: Visit Hin Ta & Hin Yai rocks (100 THB), then spend the afternoon at Lamai Beach
Late afternoon: Climb Overlap Stone viewpoint (300 THB) for sunset views over Lamai Bay
Evening: Explore Lamai's smaller nightlife strip — more relaxed than Chaweng

days.title: North Coast & Fisherman's Village
activities: Morning: Visit Wat Khunaram to see the mummified monk (free) — a surreal 15-minute cultural stop
Mid-morning: Head to Maenam Beach for a quiet beach session — golden sand, calm waters, minimal crowds
Lunch: Pad krapow moo (basil pork stir-fry) at a Maenam beachside restaurant (60–100 THB)
Afternoon: Thai massage at a Maenam or Bophut beach spa (300–500 THB for one hour)

Evening: Fisherman's Village in Bophut — if it's Friday, catch the famous Walking Street Market (5–11 PM) with food stalls, live music, and fire dancers. Otherwise, dine at one of Bophut's excellent waterfront restaurants

transport.fromBangkok: Bangkok Airways operates 15+ daily flights from Bangkok Suvarnabhumi to Samui Airport (USM) — 1 hour flight time, 3,500–8,000 THB one-way depending on season. Bangkok Airways has a monopoly on USM, making flights pricier than mainland airports. Budget alternative: fly AirAsia, Nok Air, or Thai Lion Air to Surat Thani (URT) for as low as 1,000 THB one-way, then take a shuttle bus to Don Sak pier (1 hour, included in combined tickets) and a Seatran Discovery or Lomprayah ferry to Koh Samui (1.5 hours). Combined flight+bus+ferry packages from Surat Thani cost 1,000–2,000 THB total. Overnight VIP buses from Bangkok Southern Terminal take 12–14 hours including ferry (700–1,000 THB). Trains from Bangkok Hua Lamphong to Surat Thani take 10–12 hours (from 500 THB sleeper) with onward ferry connection.
localTransport: Songthaews (shared pickup trucks): Run along the ring road for 40–100 THB per person. The cheapest way around. Agree on price before boarding.

Scooter rental: 200–350 THB/day — the most popular and practical way to explore. The ring road circles the island in about 1 hour. International driving permit technically required. Helmets mandatory.
Grab: Available but less reliable than Bangkok or Chiang Mai. Surge pricing common at night near Chaweng. Typical rides 100–300 THB.
Taxi: No meters — negotiate fixed prices before departing. Airport to Chaweng 400–600 THB, Chaweng to Lamai 300–400 THB.
Car rental: From 800 THB/day with insurance. Recommended for families or those uncomfortable on scooters. Drives on the left.
Ferries: Seatran Discovery and Lomprayah run from Nathon and Maenam piers to Koh Phangan (30 min, 200–350 THB) and Koh Tao (2 hours, 600–900 THB).

mustTry: Curried crab — local specialty with fish paste, crab marrow, and betel leaves in coconut curry (200–400 THB at seafood restaurants)
Wai khua — southern Thai coconut curry with octopus or squid, pink from shrimp paste and heavy on fresh herbs
Yellow curry with local grouper — puckery southern Thai curry with fresh-caught fish and sometimes coconut shoots
Pad krapow moo — ground pork stir-fried with holy basil, garlic, and chili, served over rice with a crispy fried egg (50–80 THB)
Khao man gai — tender boiled chicken on fragrant rice with garlic-chili-ginger sauce and chicken broth (50–80 THB)
Stuffed squid — squid filled with sticky rice, fried in squid ink with aromatic herbs — a Samui street food specialty
Tom kha gai — coconut chicken soup with galangal, lemongrass, and kaffir lime leaves — milder than tom yum but deeply flavored
Fresh coconut ice cream — made from the island's abundant coconut palms, served in the shell at beach stalls (40–60 THB)
foodAreas: Nathon night market — the most authentic and cheapest local food scene on the island, genuine southern Thai dishes at local prices
Fisherman's Village Friday Market — excellent street food alongside live music and crafts (every Friday 5–11 PM)
Laem Din market, Chaweng — central night market with a mix of Thai and tourist-friendly food stalls
Bophut waterfront restaurants — Samui's best dining concentration (Zazen, Catch, 2 Fishes)
Lamai Beach road — casual seafood restaurants with fresh-catch menus at reasonable prices
tips: Koh Samui cuisine leans southern Thai — spicier and more intense than central Thai food. Say 'pet nit noi' (a little spicy) if unsure about your heat tolerance.
Nathon town food is 40–60% cheaper than beachfront Chaweng restaurants and more authentic.
Fresh seafood is best at night markets where it's cooked to order — avoid pre-made tourist buffet restaurants.
The island's coconut plantations mean coconut-based dishes (curries, ice cream, desserts) are a genuine local specialty here.
For the best southern Thai experience, eat at restaurants where locals outnumber tourists.

etiquette: Cover shoulders and knees when visiting temples (Big Buddha, Wat Plai Laem, Wat Khunaram) — free sarongs available at main temples
Remove shoes before entering any temple building or Thai home
Don't touch monks — women should step aside on narrow paths to let monks pass without contact
Never point your feet at Buddha images or people when sitting — considered deeply disrespectful
The Thai monarchy is protected by lese-majeste laws — never make negative comments about the royal family
Stand during the national anthem played at 8 AM and 6 PM in public areas and before movies in cinemas
Topless sunbathing is illegal on all Thai beaches and can result in fines
Tipping is not expected but appreciated — 20–50 THB for restaurant meals, 50–100 THB for massage

budget.budget: 800–1,500 THB/day (~$22–42 USD): Hostel dorm 300–500 THB, street food meals 50–100 THB, free beach days, songthaew transport 40–100 THB, 1-hour Thai massage 300 THB. Samui is slightly pricier than mainland Thailand due to island logistics, but still very affordable for budget travelers, especially in Lamai or Maenam.
budget.midRange: 2,500–5,000 THB/day (~$70–140 USD): Mid-range hotel 1,500–3,500 THB, mix of street food and restaurants 300–700 THB, one tour or activity 1,500–3,000 THB, Grab transport 100–300 THB. The sweet spot for couples and first-timers who want comfort without breaking the bank.
budget.luxury: 10,000–30,000+ THB/day (~$280–840+ USD): Luxury villa resort 8,000–25,000 THB, fine dining and beach clubs 2,000–5,000 THB, private island tours 5,000–15,000 THB, spa treatments 3,000–8,000 THB. Samui's luxury scene rivals anywhere in Southeast Asia with private pool villas and world-class spas.
